channel.title: Comments for page "Underread and Underrated"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Underread and Underrated"

item.title: Re: What constitutes Young and Old?
item.content:encoded: You're welcome! Thank you for reaching out to the page curator!

item.title: Re: What constitutes Young and Old?
item.content:encoded:
Wow thank you so much, didn't expect such a quick response.

I will look into doing just that, thank you once again. I see that in the second page it says anyone can edit, but I will contact him before any edit is even done.

item.title: Re: What constitutes Young and Old?
item.content:encoded:
Based on the coding for the automatically generated lists, looks like the timeframe is the page being created in the last 180 days.

I feel, that there should be a little footnote explaining the difference.

For something like this, I recommend reaching out directly to the staff in charge of this page to let them know about the proposed edit. There's a much better chance the matter will be addressed quickly if they are directly notified (as opposed to being notified whenever they happen to see this comment in this thread).

item.title: What constitutes Young and Old?
item.content:encoded: Is the classification for young a month, year, six months? I'm sort of confused as to what the time difference between the two are. And if there is a given time frame, I feel, that there should be a little footnote explaining the difference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As the person with the original high-quality files for Finn the Falcon, should I link them somewhere?

item.title: Re: Underread and Underrated is open for business, baby!
item.content:encoded: Very good job reviving and revitalizing this project. I’m excited to see where it goes!

item.title: Underread and Underrated is open for business, baby!
(no title)
item.content:encoded: Edited the 19th review article, since there's a new 3537 which I'm the author of.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Addressed, thank you!

item.title: Typo
item.content:encoded: Hey, currently the top of the page has "as at" instead of "as of"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed SCP under review since the last one was deleted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been more than a month since the last change. So I have changed it, and it is SCP-3537. Go and vote on it when you get the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've changed the article under review to SCP-3402. When you get a chance, please take a look, and vote if you liked or disliked it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have changed the article in review to SCP-1563. When you get the chance, go take a look, and go vote on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've changed the under review article to SCP-3669. If you have a chance, please take a look.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been a little while since the last update. SCP-520 is now under review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's been a couple of weeks since this has been changed. I've changed it to SCP-3483. Check it out and vote on it when you get the chance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because psul has said that anyone can change the skip under review, I've gone ahead and done just that. I've changed it to SCP-2834, go vote on it if you haven't already!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel terrible for missing this entire thread for so long. Thanks for clearing the permission thing up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, it's just a shame. I get it, SCP articles are what this whole site is based on and they deserve the most attention - but there's some great Tales out there that are worth reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …It probably wouldn't get as much attention. >.>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This may have already been discussed, but would it be worthwhile to have a similar scheme for Tales? I feel it's harder to get votes for Tales for various reasons (not impossible, don't get me wrong! I know there's some very highly rated Tales) and it would help draw attention to Tales that are trying to start a new canon/characters/anomalous activities for example.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for clearing that up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the record, I'm very happy with people changing the skip under review. I have been (semi-deliberately) not updating it, because I don't want this to be a one-person project. I still strongly encourage others to post reviews and draw attention to the project and to skips within the category.

I've updated the bullet points to expressly allow updates to the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought anyone was allowed to change it without permission?

None of the bullet points listed after the "Here's what you can do:" bit specifically refer to the top box. I personally thought that the referred to the tables in the tab block at the bottom.

That said, it's still polite to nudge a page owner if you're updating something that they usually handle. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought anyone was allowed to change it without permission?

This is not a one-person show. This is not a staff or staff-sanctioned project, so everyone should feel free to contribute - you don't need permission, and the only rules that apply are the existing forum/crit rules. Here's what you can do:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's good that you asked beforehand, though it might also be good if you send a PM over to psul about the update, seeing as this project was spearheaded by him if I recall correctly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed it to 3046 because it was old and had few votes. I am now going to post it on Reddit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh, yeah, it's been two months, that should definitely happen. D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you guys mind if I change the under review scip? It has been quite a while since it was last changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m not gonna lie, I love this idea, it gives publicity to relatively unknown or under developed SCPs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Announcing that we've updated the page to focus on the current review - it's now SCP-3535.

As previously noted, anyone should feel free to update the "currently under review" section if it hasn't been changed in a few days - that should help keep up momentum. Just be sensible, and don't pick one of your own skips, hey?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I recall correctly at the time I did the review the format was given as something like total votes(total upvotes), so that's what I put there. Noted in my review that the rating was +17 with 24 upvotes and 9 downvotes, though, so however we want to list them, those are the stats.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, it's SCP book club! By far one of the coolest initiatives on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the initial vote count for SCP-2587 is borked: scpper shows that it never reached above +20.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Belated, but it's on the front page news now! Feel free to let me know if you'd like the blurb edited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm positively surprised. Ever since I was introduced to the SCP wiki, I've always felt this missing something, which I have never been able to identify at all. But upon knowing that something like this is happening now, that missing something have somehow lessen. Job well done! ++1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No strong preference on a blurb - thanks for putting it up on the front page!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good to see this up! psul, if you're okay with this being featured in the front page site news, do you have a blurb you'd like to use for the entry?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a voting module to the page. Other staff might feel otherwise (and this isn't really a staff post either), but I can't see a compelling reason to leave it off the page. It's positively rated, as it should be. Good luck on the project, I might help out myself when I have some free time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I could claim that it was like "under 21"-type sports which often allow people of the named age to compete, but it's probably just easier to amend the listpages module (which I have now done)!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just a note, the listpages is currently looking up articles with a rating <=30, which means this is basically Young and under 31.

Dunno if that was intended or not.

item.title: Refining Yau30 processes
item.content:encoded:
A quick suggestion for those helping with the "Young and under 30" project - if you're reviewing an article for the project, please title your post to refer to the project, and call for others to review as well. I hadn't realised that people had put several articles up for review until I looked at the second tab again, because the forum posts didn't refer to the project.

And I'd suggest that we don't have more than one article under review at a time (around one article every 2-3 days) - I'd like this project to focus as much of the attention of the "Recent Posts" crowd on a single article as possible, otherwise it won't have as clear an effect on votes and feedback as everyone would like.

That said, let's leave the current five or so in place for a week or two, and work through them before moving on to the next.

I applaud everyone's enthusiasm so far - just hoping to refine the process slightly to make sure we get best value from this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, this is a cool idea. :) I've got a couple articles that fit the bill, so I hope they get picked up! (Won't mention which, as it would likely be in bad taste.)
